term_inventory_hebrew.meaning:
1) kidneys
1a) of physical organ (lit.)
1b) of seat of emotion and affection (fig.)
1c) of sacrificial animals
2) (TWOT) reins

term_inventory_greek.meaning: to share knowledge with; to be conscious (of oneself) aware; a defective verb that is actually perfect in form but present in meaning, to share in the knowledge of a thing; to be privy to, Acts 5:2; to be conscious; oudén sunoida, to have a clear conscience, 1Cor. 4:4
term_inventory_greek.lsj_entry: sunoida: perfect with present sense. Know something about a person, especially as a potential witness for or against him. With dative of persons: share the knowledge of something with somebody, to be implicated in or privy to it. With dative of things: know something about a thing. Know well (as from one's own knowledge). To be privy to. __V to syneidós: complicity; consciousness. __V.2 conscience (hypo syneidotos eparresiazeto agathou). Also: as you may think proper.

verse_records.verse_text: and with his wife's knowledge he kept back for himself some of the proceeds and brought only a part of it and laid it at the apostles' feet.
When he realized this, he went to the house of Mary, the mother of John whose other name was Mark, where many were gathered together and were praying.
they learned of it and fled to Lystra and Derbe, cities of Lycaonia, and to the surrounding country,
And you shall take all the fat that covers the entrails, and the long lobe of the liver, and the two kidneys with the fat that is on them, and burn them on the altar.

You shall also take the fat from the ram and the fat tail and the fat that covers the entrails, and the long lobe of the liver and the two kidneys with the fat that is on them, and the right thigh (for it is a ram of ordination),
and the two kidneys with the fat that is on them at the loins, and the long lobe of the liver that he shall remove with the kidneys.
and the two kidneys with the fat that is on them at the loins and the long lobe of the liver that he shall remove with the kidneys.
and the two kidneys with the fat that is on them at the loins and the long lobe of the liver that he shall remove with the kidneys.

the two kidneys with the fat that is on them at the loins, and the long lobe of the liver that he shall remove with the kidneys.
And he took all the fat that was on the entrails and the long lobe of the liver and the two kidneys with their fat, and Moses burned them on the altar.
Then he took the fat and the fat tail and all the fat that was on the entrails and the long lobe of the liver and the two kidneys with their fat and the right thigh,
But the fat and the kidneys and the long lobe of the liver from the sin offering he burned on the altar, as the LORD commanded Moses.
But the fat pieces of the ox and of the ram, the fat tail and that which covers the entrails and the kidneys and the long lobe of the liver—
Curds from the herd, and milk from the flock, with fat of lambs, rams of Bashan and goats, with the very finest of the wheat— and you drank foaming wine made from the blood of the grape.
his archers surround me. He slashes open my kidneys and does not spare; he pours out my gall on the ground.
whom I shall see for myself, and my eyes shall behold, and not another. My heart faints within me!
Oh, let the evil of the wicked come to an end, and may you establish the righteous— you who test the minds and hearts, O righteous God!
I bless the LORD who gives me counsel; in the night also my heart instructs me.
Prove me, O LORD, and try me; test my heart and my mind.
When my soul was embittered, when I was pricked in heart,
For you formed my inward parts; you knitted me together in my mother's womb.
My inmost being will exult when your lips speak what is right.

The LORD has a sword; it is sated with blood; it is gorged with fat, with the blood of lambs and goats, with the fat of the kidneys of rams. For the LORD has a sacrifice in Bozrah, a great slaughter in the land of Edom.
But, O LORD of hosts, who judges righteously, who tests the heart and the mind, let me see your vengeance upon them, for to you have I committed my cause.
You plant them, and they take root; they grow and produce fruit; you are near in their mouth and far from their heart.
I the LORD search the heart and test the mind, to give every man according to his ways, according to the fruit of his deeds.
O LORD of hosts, who tests the righteous, who sees the heart and the mind, let me see your vengeance upon them, for to you have I committed my cause.
He drove into my kidneys the arrows of his quiver;

cross_registry_links.note: suneidēsis (G4893) is owned by guilt (073). In the guilt registry it covers the moral-liability sense; conscience (026) accesses the same term for the inner-faculty and consciousness senses. The two registries illuminate different aspects of the same lexeme.

suneidō (G4894) and suneidēsis (G4893) are cognates — the verb and noun of the same root. The guilt registry owns the noun; conscience owns the verb. Phase 2 should treat these as a matched pair across registries.

data_quality_flags.description: suneidō occurs about 4x. Only 3 verse records in source file. The primary conscience verse cited in the header (1Cor 4:4 — oudén sunoida, 'I am not aware of anything against myself') is absent from the STEP verse list. Assessments based on Acts verses only; 1Cor 4:4 sense noted in meaning block from STEP definition.

kil.yah occurs about 31x; 22 verses recorded (71% coverage). Above 20% threshold — no data gap. Flagged for awareness. Many of the unrecorded verses are additional Leviticus sacrificial-fat references with no inner-being content.

kil.yah presents a somatic-inner-being bridge: the physical kidney is also the OT locus of emotion, affection, and deep inner reflection (Psa 7:9, 16:7, 26:2, 73:21, 139:13; Jer 11:20, 17:10; Pro 23:16). This is the most explicit anatomical seat-of-consciousness term in the Hebrew corpus. The majority of the verse set (Lev/Exo/Deu) is sacrificial-literal; the Psalms/Prophets subset carries the inner-being content. Phase 2 should separate these two verse clusters for analysis.

suneidēsis (G4893) appears in the registry header as a primary conscience term and has 30+ verse records in the source document. It is an XREF (owned by guilt 073). Full verse data is held in the guilt registry. The suneidēsis data provided inline in the source document is not extracted here — it belongs to registry 073. Conscience 026 accesses it via XREF only.
